Label: Map Visibility
Notes: Global toggle on the tab header. Shows/hides all reverbs on the Map tab.
Hover help text in the status bar: Show or hide reverb markers on the Map tab.

Label: Edit on Map
enum: Edit on Map ; Edit on Map ON
Notes: Tab-level toggle, not persisted. Allows dragging reverb positions on the Map.
Hover help text in the status bar: Enable editing reverb positions from the Map tab.

Label: Solo Reverbs
Notes: Tab-level toggle. Long-press to toggle. Mutes all direct signal paths and keeps the reverb feeds/returns. Exclusive with Mute Pre and Mute Post.
Hover help text in the status bar: Long-press to solo all reverbs: direct signal is muted, reverb is kept.

Label: Mute Pre
Notes: Tab-level toggle. Long-press to toggle. Silences the input-to-reverb feed sends. Exclusive with Solo Reverbs and Mute Post.
Hover help text in the status bar: Long-press to silence the reverb feed sends.

Label: Mute Post
Notes: Tab-level toggle. Long-press to toggle. Skips the reverb return to the output mix. Exclusive with Solo Reverbs and Mute Pre.
Hover help text in the status bar: Long-press to skip reverb returns in the output mix.

Label: Selected Reverb
Notes: Max bounded by the Reverb Channels count in System Config (up to 16). Display channel name on the switches.
column17: Type R to switch to the Reverb Tab. Prompt in the status bar for 5 seconds to type a number and then return to select the corresponding reverb channel.

Label: Reverb Name
Hover help text in the status bar: Displayed Reverb Channel Name (editable).

Label: Attenuation
Hover help text in the status bar: Reverb Channel Attenuation.

Label: Delay/Latency comp.
Notes: Highlight 'Latency Compensation' for negative values or 'Delay' for positive values.
Hover help text in the status bar: Reverb Channel Delay (positive values) or Latency Compensation (negative values).

Label: Coordinate Mode
enum: XYZ ; r theta Z ; r theta phi
Notes: Controls how Position X/Y/Z are displayed and edited: Cartesian, Cylindrical, or Spherical. Storage remains Cartesian internally.
Hover help text in the status bar: Select Cartesian, Cylindrical, or Spherical coordinates for this reverb.

Label: Position X
Hover help text in the status bar: Reverb Channel Position in Width.
Keyboard shortcuts: Left and Right Arrows to nudge to the left (--) or right (++) in 0.1m increments

Label: Position Y
Hover help text in the status bar: Reverb Channel Position in Depth.
Keyboard shortcuts: Up and Down Arrows to nudge to the upstage (++) or downstage (--) in 0.1m increments

Label: Position Z
Hover help text in the status bar: Reverb Channel Position in Height.
Keyboard shortcuts: Page Up and Page Down to nudge to the up (++) or down (--) in 0.1m increments

Label: Return Offset X
Hover help text in the status bar: Reverb Return Channel Position Offset in Width.
Keyboard shortcuts: Left and Right Arrows to nudge to the left (--) or right (++) in 0.1m increments if holding the [shift] key

Label: Return Offset Y
Hover help text in the status bar: Reverb Return Channel Position Offset in Depth.
Keyboard shortcuts: Up and Down Arrows to nudge to the upstage (++) or downstage (--) in 0.1m increments if holding the [shift] key

Label: Return Offset Z
Hover help text in the status bar: Reverb Return Channel Position Offset in Height.
Keyboard shortcuts: Page Up and Page Down to nudge to the up (++) or down (--) in 0.1m increments if holding the [shift] key

Label: Orientation
Hover help text in the status bar: Reverb Channel Horizontal Orientation (0 deg = towards the audience in a frontal configuration).

Label: Angle On
Notes: angleOn + angleOff <= 180; adjust angleOff if angleOn is adjusted and sum is above 180.
Hover help text in the status bar: Reverb Channel Will Amplify Objects in this Angle in its Back.

Label: Angle Off
Notes: angleOn + angleOff <= 180; adjust angleOn if angleOff is adjusted and sum is above 180.
Hover help text in the status bar: Reverb Channel Will Not Amplify Objects in this Angle in Front of it.

Label: Pitch
Hover help text in the status bar: Reverb Channel Vertical Orientation used to Determine which Objects get amplified.

Label: HF Damping
Hover help text in the status bar: Loss of High Frequency Depending on Distance from Object to Reverb.

Label: Minimum Latency Enable
Hover help text in the status bar: Disables Minimal Latency Mode for Selected Reverb.

Label: Live Source Attenuation Enable
Hover help text in the status bar: Disables Live Source Attenuation for Selected Reverb.

Label: Distance Attenuation Percentage
Hover help text in the status bar: Ratio of Distance Attenuation for Selected Reverb.

Label: EQ Enabled
Notes: Per-channel parameter.
Hover help text in the status bar: Enable or Disables Equalisation of the Reverb Channel.

Label: EQ Flatten
Notes: Long-press to reset all 4 bands of the Pre-EQ on the selected channel to defaults.
Hover help text in the status bar: Long-press to flatten the Pre-EQ on this channel.

Label: EQ Band Reset
Notes: For each of 4 EQ bands. Long-press to reset that single band to its default shape/freq/gain/Q.
Hover help text in the status bar: Long-press to reset this EQ band.

Label: EQ Display
Notes: Per-channel EQ graph. Click a marker to select a band, drag horizontally to set frequency, vertically to set gain, mouse-wheel to adjust Q.
Hover help text in the status bar: Drag the EQ band markers; use the mouse wheel on a marker to adjust its Q.

Label: EQ Shape
enum: Low Cut (1) ; Low Shelf (2) ; Peak/Notch (3) ; Band Pass (6) ; High Shelf (4) ; High Cut (5)
Notes: For each of 4 EQ bands. Default shapes: Band 1=Band Pass, Band 2=Peak/Notch, Band 3=Peak/Notch, Band 4=High Shelf. Default frequencies: 200 / 800 / 2000 / 5000 Hz. OFF state is handled by the separate EQ Band Toggle, not by an enum value.
Hover help text in the status bar: Select the EQ Mode for Band <Band ID> of the Reverb Channel.

Label: EQ Frequency
Notes: For each of 4 EQ bands. Defaults per band: 200 / 800 / 2000 / 5000 Hz. Greyed out but editable when the Band Toggle is OFF.
Hover help text in the status bar: Select the EQ Frequency for Band <Band ID> of the Reverb Channel.

Label: EQ Gain
Notes: For each of 4 EQ bands. Hidden for Low Cut / High Cut / Band Pass shapes. Greyed out but editable when the Band Toggle is OFF.
Hover help text in the status bar: Select the EQ Gain/Attenuation for Band <Band ID> of the Reverb Channel.

Label: EQ Q
Notes: For each of 4 EQ bands. Greyed out but editable when the Band Toggle is OFF.
Hover help text in the status bar: Select the EQ Q for Band <Band ID> of the Reverb Channel.

Label: EQ Slope
Notes: For each of 4 EQ bands. Applies to shelf shapes. Greyed out but editable when the Band Toggle is OFF.
Hover help text in the status bar: Select the EQ Slope for Band <Band ID> of the Reverb Channel.

Label: Compressor Bypass
Notes: Global parameter.
Hover help text in the status bar: Enable or disable the pre-compressor for the reverb feed signal.

Label: Compressor GR Meter
Notes: Read-only real-time gain-reduction display (blue). Not a control.
Hover help text in the status bar: Real-time pre-compressor gain reduction.

Label: Compressor Threshold
Notes: Global parameter.
Hover help text in the status bar: Pre-compressor threshold level (-60 to 0 dB).

Label: Compressor Ratio
Notes: Global parameter.
Hover help text in the status bar: Pre-compressor ratio (1:1 to 20:1).

Label: Compressor Attack
Notes: Global parameter.
Hover help text in the status bar: Pre-compressor attack time (0.1 - 100 ms).

Label: Compressor Release
Notes: Global parameter.
Hover help text in the status bar: Pre-compressor release time (10 - 1000 ms).

Label: Algorithm Type
Notes: Global parameter.
Hover help text in the status bar: Select the reverb algorithm: SDN (Scattering Delay Network), FDN (Feedback Delay Network), or IR (Impulse Response).

Label: Algorithm Help
Notes: Info button. Opens a help card with explanations for each algorithm.
Hover help text in the status bar: Show the reverb algorithm help card.

Label: RT60
Notes: Visible for SDN and FDN.
Hover help text in the status bar: Reverb decay time RT60 (0.2 - 8.0 seconds).

Label: RT60 Low Mult
Notes: Visible for SDN and FDN.
Hover help text in the status bar: Low frequency RT60 multiplier (0.1 - 9.0x).

Label: RT60 High Mult
Notes: Visible for SDN and FDN.
Hover help text in the status bar: High frequency RT60 multiplier (0.1 - 9.0x).

Label: Crossover Low
Notes: Visible for SDN and FDN.
Hover help text in the status bar: Low crossover frequency for 3-band decay (50 - 500 Hz).

Label: Crossover High
Notes: Visible for SDN and FDN.
Hover help text in the status bar: High crossover frequency for 3-band decay (1 - 10 kHz).

Label: Diffusion
Notes: Visible for SDN and FDN.
Hover help text in the status bar: Diffusion amount controlling echo density (0 - 100%).

Label: SDN Scale
Notes: Visible only for SDN.
Hover help text in the status bar: SDN inter-node delay scale factor (0.5 - 4.0x).

Label: FDN Size
Notes: Visible only for FDN.
Hover help text in the status bar: FDN delay line size multiplier (0.5 - 2.0x).

Label: IR File
Notes: Visible only for IR.
Hover help text in the status bar: Load an impulse response WAV file for convolution.

Label: IR Trim
Notes: Visible only for IR. Max is the loaded IR file's duration in ms (dynamic per file).
Hover help text in the status bar: Trim the start of the impulse response.

Label: IR Length
Notes: Visible only for IR. Max is the loaded IR file's duration in seconds (dynamic per file).
Hover help text in the status bar: Maximum impulse response length.

Label: Per-node IR
enum: Per-node IR OFF ; Per-node IR ON
Notes: Visible only for IR.
Hover help text in the status bar: Use a separate IR for each reverb node, or share one IR.

Label: Wet Level
Notes: Always visible.
Hover help text in the status bar: Wet/dry mix level for reverb output (-60 to +12 dB).

Label: Post EQ Enabled
Notes: Global parameter.
Hover help text in the status bar: Enable or disable post-processing EQ.

Label: Post EQ Flatten
Notes: Long-press to reset all 4 bands of the Post-EQ to defaults.
Hover help text in the status bar: Long-press to flatten the Post-EQ.

Label: Post EQ Band Reset
Notes: For each of 4 EQ bands. Long-press to reset that single band to its default.
Hover help text in the status bar: Long-press to reset this EQ band.

Label: Post EQ Display
Notes: Global EQ graph. Click a marker to select a band, drag horizontally to set frequency, vertically to set gain, mouse-wheel to adjust Q.
Hover help text in the status bar: Drag the EQ band markers; use the mouse wheel on a marker to adjust its Q.

Label: Post EQ Shape
enum: Low Cut (1) ; Low Shelf (2) ; Peak/Notch (3) ; Band Pass (6) ; High Shelf (4) ; High Cut (5)
Notes: For each of 4 EQ bands. Default shapes: Band 1=Band Pass, Band 2=Peak/Notch, Band 3=Peak/Notch, Band 4=High Shelf. Default frequencies: 200 / 800 / 2000 / 5000 Hz. Global parameter. OFF state is handled by the separate EQ Band Toggle, not by an enum value.
Hover help text in the status bar: Post-EQ Band <Band ID> filter shape.

Label: Post EQ Frequency
Notes: Global, 4 bands. Defaults per band: 200 / 800 / 2000 / 5000 Hz.
Hover help text in the status bar: Post-EQ Band <Band ID> frequency (20 Hz - 20 kHz).

Label: Post EQ Gain
Notes: Global, 4 bands. Hidden for Low Cut / High Cut / Band Pass shapes.
Hover help text in the status bar: Post-EQ Band <Band ID> gain (-24 to +24 dB).

Label: Post EQ Q
Notes: Global, 4 bands.
Hover help text in the status bar: Post-EQ Band <Band ID> Q factor (0.1 - 20.0).

Label: Post EQ Slope
Notes: Global, 4 bands. Applies to shelf shapes.
Hover help text in the status bar: Post-EQ Band <Band ID> slope (0.1 - 20.0).

Label: Expander Bypass
Notes: Global parameter.
Hover help text in the status bar: Enable or disable the post-expander for the reverb return signal.

Label: Expander GR Meter
Notes: Read-only real-time gain-reduction display (purple). Not a control.
Hover help text in the status bar: Real-time post-expander gain reduction.

Label: Expander Threshold
Notes: Global parameter.
Hover help text in the status bar: Post-expander threshold level (-80 to -10 dB).

Label: Expander Ratio
Notes: Global parameter. Ratio is 1:N (downward expansion).
Hover help text in the status bar: Post-expander ratio (1:1 to 1:8).

Label: Expander Attack
Notes: Global parameter.
Hover help text in the status bar: Post-expander attack time (0.1 - 50 ms).

Label: Expander Release
Notes: Global parameter.
Hover help text in the status bar: Post-expander release time (50 - 2000 ms).

Label: Mutes
Notes: As many toggles as outputs.
Hover help text in the status bar: Mute Selectively the Output of a Reverb to a Speaker.

Label: Mute Macros
enum: Mute Macro Select ; MUTE ALL ; UNMUTE ALL ; INVERT MUTES ; MUTE ODD ; MUTE EVEN ; MUTE ARRAY 1 ; UNMUTE ARRAY 1 ; MUTE ARRAY 2 ; UNMUTE ARRAY 2 ; MUTE ARRAY 3 ; UNMUTE ARRAY 3 ; MUTE ARRAY 4 ; UNMUTE ARRAY 4 ; MUTE ARRAY 5 ; UNMUTE ARRAY 5 ; MUTE ARRAY 6 ; UNMUTE ARRAY 6 ; MUTE ARRAY 7 ; UNMUTE ARRAY 7 ; MUTE ARRAY 8 ; UNMUTE ARRAY 8 ; MUTE ARRAY 9 ; UNMUTE ARRAY 9 ; MUTE ARRAY 10 ; UNMUTE ARRAY 10
Notes: Close panel after selection. Temporary switches.
Hover help text in the status bar: Mute Macros for Fast Muting and Unmuting of Arrays.

Label: Store Reverb Configuration
Notes: Store Reverb Configuration to file (makes a backup and shows confirmation in the status bar).
Hover help text in the status bar: Store Reverb Configuration to file (overwrite with confirmation).

Label: Reload Reverb Configuration
Notes: Reload Reverb Configuration from file (confirmation in status bar).
Hover help text in the status bar: Reload Reverb Configuration from file (with confirmation).

Label: Reload Reverb Config Backup
Notes: Reload Reverb Configuration from backup file (confirmation in status bar).
Hover help text in the status bar: Reload Reverb Configuration from backup file (with confirmation).

Label: Import Reverb Configuration
Notes: Import Reverb Configuration from file (with file-explorer window).
Hover help text in the status bar: Import Reverb Configuration from file (with file explorer window).

Label: Export Reverb Configuration
Notes: Export Reverb Configuration to file (with file-explorer window).
Hover help text in the status bar: Export Reverb Configuration to file (with file explorer window).
